pi_items.source: Blender EEVEE documentation confirms 64 is a standard render sample count for EEVEE TAA (Temporal Anti-Aliasing) rendering, offering a balance between quality and performance
Blender EEVEE documentation confirms 32 is a reasonable and commonly used viewport sample count for real-time viewport performance

Blender EEVEE GTAO (Ground Truth Ambient Occlusion) distance default is 0.2m, but 1.0m is a valid and reasonable higher distance value. Verifier.py allows 0.9-1.1m range, confirming this value is within expected parameters
1920x1080 is the standard Full HD resolution (1080p), confirmed as industry standard
Path is consistent across task.json metadata, task description, export_result.sh (line 7), and setup_task.sh (line 17)
Path is consistent across task.json metadata, task description, export_result.sh (line 6), and setup_task.sh (line 18)
URL verified as accessible and returns HTTP 200 with content-type application/zip (3,009,385 bytes). File contains valid BMW27.blend (3,148,419 bytes, gzip-compressed)
BMW27.blend is a Cycles-based benchmark scene (from Blender official demo files). Task description and setup_task.sh confirm initial state is Cycles (line 54: scene.render.engine = 'CYCLES')

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Blender BMW27 demo file (3.1 MB gzip-compressed .blend file from https://download.blender.org/demo/test/BMW27.blend.zip), which is a publicly available benchmark scene originally designed for Cycles rendering. All metadata values are verified: render samples (64), viewport samples (32), AO distance (1.0m, within expected 0.9-1.1m range), resolution (1920x1080 Full HD), and output file paths are internally consistent. The task requires converting a Cycles-based scene to EEVEE with specific quality settings.
